BERT Paper Notes — "Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding" (Devlin et al., 2018): BERT introduced a masked language modeling pre-training objective that enables the model to learn bidirectional context from unlabeled text, unlike GPT-style left-to-right language models. The model is pre-trained on two tasks: predicting randomly masked tokens (MLM) and next sentence prediction (NSP), using a large corpus of BooksCorpus and English Wikipedia. Fine-tuning BERT on downstream tasks like question answering, textual entailment, and named entity recognition achieved state-of-the-art results across 11 NLP benchmarks simultaneously. The key insight is that deep bidirectional representations are more powerful than left-to-right or shallow bidirectional models for most language understanding tasks.
